spawn.probe: exact two-place Tate coefficient audit at S={inf,2,3}, u=1, t=1/2 (Arb 512-bit); any stabilized nonzero mixed coefficient > 2^-160 kills it

cut_set.statement: Greenlit C9 formulation of the causal square: C^J_{u,t} = T_{u,t} o mult(m_d) with m_d the target-independent minimal polynomial of the boundary-germ spectral operator A_d; B(m_d Q, m_d R) = <T(m_d Q), T(m_d R)> exactly on the dense algebraic Tate core.
DeltaRiemannCriticalStripCert: delta-framework critical-strip certificate supporting the ledger point-balance coupling.

Pole-crossing Schur rung v2: Theta_h(s)=xi(s-h)/xi(s+h) at h=1/8, anchor 1+h, aperture 9/16, positivity carried only at kernel level as the exterior-square Schur complement C_h(s,t). Gate currently emits IDENTITY_ONLY (Stage-0 identity bankable; W_23 comparison open).

Committed missing lemma: explicit Hilbert space H and complex-linear C_{u,t} built only from FE/(co-)Poisson transport, Lambda(n)>=0, Stirling, Vasyunin inner products, BC rewrites (no RH-equivalent input) with B_{u,t}(Q,R) = <C Q, C R> polarizing Q -> Re W(pgTest(Q,u,t)), t-uniform. Diagonal yields WeilPositivityPGDialed. NO Lean declaration exists yet (verified absent 2026-07-10).

walls.kills: OddThetaSWCutoffCauchyDomination provable RH-free by exact identity/spectral symbol
walls.constraint: Any RH-free positivity certificate on the odd channel would be insensitive to planted strip quartets; the form measurably detects them. The complex zero expansion itself consumes no zero-realness. Zero-realness first enters only when that expansion is converted to nonnegative squares. Parity selection is the routing constraint: the odd sector is blind to purely imaginary frequency zeros, so their exclusion is a separate node.
